reasoning:
The "Coalition of the Willing" was announced in early 2025 primarily by France and the UK to form a reassurance force that would potentially deploy to Ukraine as part of a post-war security guarantee, or following a lasting ceasefire. As of late 2025, 26 nations have pledged to this effort, offering eventual troop deployments over land, sea, and air. However, these commitments explicitly stipulate that troops will only be deployed *after* an enduring ceasefire or comprehensive peace deal is firmly established. 

A temporary limited 30-day ceasefire was agreed to in mid-March 2025 alongside a broader push led by the Trump administration; however, this evidently did not culminate in a final peace plan. Throughout the rest of the year, there has been no comprehensive ceasefire agreement, with Russia continuing to decline substantial ceasefire and peace deal negotiations as recently as September. The conditions necessary for the reassurance force to deploy are fundamentally unmet. In addition, the crowd forecast from INFER on October 30, 2025 positioned the probability at just 0.01 (1%), indicating deep confidence among attentive forecasters that no deployment logic or Edge-case loop-holes (like drone interception by Coalition from international waters) applied or had any imminent chance of taking place before the close of 2025. With less than two months remaining in the year, and peace talks effectively stalled, the probability of military forces deploying into Ukraine under the Command of the "Coalition of the Willing" before January 1, 2026, is exceedingly low. The lowest acceptable probability for this framework is assigned.
